strengths: Comprehensive statistical analysis with proper effect sizes (Cohen's d) and ANOVA
Clean within-tool-age-group analysis definitively separates H1 from H2
Threshold analysis provides actionable alarm limits
Proper use of v6.0 competing hypotheses protocol with discriminability assessment
Temporal lead/lag analysis strengthens causal inference
No missing data, no data quality issues

weaknesses.issue: No direct bearing condition measurement available
weaknesses.impact: Cannot confirm bearing degradation directly - only through secondary indicators (temperature, vibration)
weaknesses.mitigation: Add bearing condition monitoring (acceleration envelope analysis, acoustic emission)

weaknesses.issue: Confidence capped at 65 per protocol but evidence is unusually strong for COMPETING_SET
weaknesses.impact: Conservative - actual confidence in H1 may be higher

verification_checks.check: No silent assumption about unknown parameters

recommendations: Upgrade diagnostician from COMPETING_SET to DETERMINED if spindle bearing inspection data confirms wear pattern matching temperature/vibration profile
Add controlled experiment: replace spindle bearings at production mid-point and observe quality recovery
